# Questions in Livestream 233

Live Stream #233: Happy All Saints' Eve!!!

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=991" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:31</a> | Why does the subscriber-only chat timer keep changing? | He says they vary the wait time to throw off less sophisticated bots. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=1039" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:19</a> | Does David want to abolish the monarchy? | He says no; he thinks the monarchy still serves a stabilizing function. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=1093" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:13</a> | Is it a sin to be introverted? | He says no; he describes himself as very introverted. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=1141" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:01</a> | Is Mrs. Producer extroverted? | He says yes; she is extroverted while he is a gregarious introvert. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=1266" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:06</a> | What Halloween traditions does the Falk family have? | They mostly avoid trick-or-treating activity, hide out, and sometimes watch *Vampire Princess Miyu*. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=1350" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:30</a> | Would "pact" be an accurate translation for the ark because it covers both conspiracy and contract? | He says no; the text uses distinct terms, so he would keep the ideas separate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=1444" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:04</a> | What is true versus false worship if only God can read hearts? | He says true worship aims at loving and esteeming God, while false worship aims at self-display; God can tell the difference even when people cannot. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=1632" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:12</a> | What are your thoughts on the Ark Museum? | He says there are several "Ark museums" and he is mostly indifferent, especially about Ark Encounter. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=1877" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">31:17</a> | Did visitors perform priestly activities? | He says some visitors did if they held priestly titles; it depended on the person. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=1936" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:16</a> | What is a hill country? | He says it means a hilly region, typically the Judean hill country distinct from the coastal plain and Dead Sea basin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=2022" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:42</a> | How can I love the Lord with all my heart and soul if I am burdened by self-loathing? | He says to give that self-loathing to God too; loving God does not require a perfect inner state. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=2154" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:54</a> | In Ezekiel 14:14, is Daniel a foreign mythic figure rather than the biblical Daniel? | He leans toward Danel as a non-Israelite wise figure rather than the biblical Daniel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=2400" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:00</a> | How different is Judaism from Israelite religion? | He says modern rabbinic Judaism is shaped by life without temple sacrifice and by the Talmud, so it is not the same as ancient Israelite religion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=2679" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:39</a> | What did ancient Israelite and early Christian fasting look like? | He says it usually meant abstaining from food, not water, often from sunset to sunset, with some fasts allowing a little bread. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=2910" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:30</a> | Was fasting usually done only on a day off from work? | He says not necessarily, but serious fasting was easier for people without manual-labor jobs or with more means. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=2963" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:23</a> | In Joshua 18:10, didn't Moses already divide the land? | He says Moses gave a rough allocation, but Joshua worked out the detailed divisions at Shiloh once the land was known. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=3118" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:58</a> | In Joshua 19:15, was there a Bethlehem in Zebulun? | He says yes, but it was a different Bethlehem from Bethlehem of Judah. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=3329" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:29</a> | Did the ancient Israelites have a gesture equivalent to raising the middle finger? | He says none is known; they may have had one, but it was not recorded. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=3471" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:51</a> | Have you ever struggled making pie dough? | He says no, if you keep the butter cold, use ice water, and let the dough rest. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=3679" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:19</a> | What is the prevailing view among scientists and anthropologists on spiritual entities? | He says the fields usually treat them in material or cultural terms, though individual scholars differ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=3790" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:10</a> | Does Joshua 24:31 imply a generation after Joshua? | He says yes; the verse reflects time after Joshua's death and likely later composition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=3924" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:24</a> | What did it mean for men and women not to wear each other's clothing if both wore tunics? | He says male and female tunics had distinct styles, so the prohibition concerns cross-dressing, not identical garments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=4042" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:22</a> | In Revelation 20:12, do deeds determine whether someone is in the book of life? | He says no; the book of life determines salvation, while deeds determine rewards or punishments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=4365" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:45</a> | Why couldn't Benjamin take Jerusalem in Judges 1 when Judah had already defeated it? | He says Judah only sacked and burned it temporarily; the Jebusites rebuilt, and Benjamin later failed to finish the job. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=4542" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:42</a> | Could you do a video on Christianity and self-defense sometime? | He says yes, because Scripture has a fair amount to say on self-defense. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=4580" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:20</a> | Were Croesus's horses defeated because horses fear the smell of camels? | He says no; horses are not generally afraid of camels, though camels could still be effective cavalry because of speed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=4805" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:05</a> | Which are grumpier: horses, camels, or llamas or alpacas? | He says camels are the grumpiest of the ones he knows firsthand. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=4882" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:22</a> | Do you find value in any of Simcha Jacobovici's work? | He says no and would not recommend his work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=4961" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:41</a> | Would you like to be addressed as Melissa Scott or Dr. Mr. Goldman? | He says he does not understand the question, then says he prefers being addressed as Dr. Falk. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=4986" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:06</a> | In Judges 3:8, who is Cushan-Rishathaim? | He says the name likely points to a ruler from Aram rather than a clearly identifiable Mesopotamian king. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=5292" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:12</a> | In Judges 4:11, who is Moses's father-in-law? | He says several names appear because Moses may have had multiple marriages and thus multiple fathers-in-law. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=5459" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:59</a> | Could Jethro's priestly line have stemmed from Melchizedek? | He says no; Jethro was Midianite, while Melchizedek was tied to the Amorite or Jebusite world. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=5556" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:36</a> | Did Jethro and Melchizedek worship the same God? | He says yes, though under different names. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=5605" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:25</a> | Were the different divine names just the result of different languages? | He says not necessarily; the naming differences seem more religious than merely linguistic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=5681" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:41</a> | In Judges 5:4, Israel did not go to Edom, so what is the verse describing? | He says it describes the Lord coming from Seir or Edom, not Israel marching there. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=5858" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:38</a> | Why were the Edomites doomed? | He says pride was central, along with not helping Israel in its time of need. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=5903" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:23</a> | Does Judges 5 preserve especially ancient Hebrew vocabulary and grammar? | He says yes, the song does appear to preserve notable archaisms, especially in vocabulary. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=5971" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:31</a> | Do historians know anything more about Melchizedek's line beyond the Bible? | He says no; the Bible is basically our only direct witness. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=6046" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:46</a> | In Judges 7:25, what are the rock of Oreb and the winepress of Zeeb? | He says the rock is a landmark or escarpment, while the winepress is the low-elevation place where grapes were processed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=6250" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:44:10</a> | What are your thoughts on demon possession, modern psychology, and exorcism before baptism? | He says possession is real, but exorcism before baptism seems unnecessary because Christian commitment and the Spirit already address that problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=6701" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:41</a> | Can pastors be possessed? | He says yes, they can. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=6898" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:58</a> | Can Catholic clergy get possessed? | He says yes if they are not truly Christian; clerical status itself offers no protection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=6960" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:00</a> | What happened to the spirit beings worshiped in ancient times, and do they still influence the world? | He says some were bound, some still operate, and some still influence people and ideologies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=7103" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:23</a> | Were the Nephilim demon-possessed if they were not offspring of bad angels? | He says no; he rejects the angel-offspring view and sees the Nephilim as the result of a broken human religious and sexual union. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=7183" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:43</a> | Do you have a video on the Nephilim topic? | He says there is a short and that a full Monday teaching video on the topic is coming soon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=7272" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:12</a> | When were the Amorites driven out of Syria? | He says the Arameans displaced them around the tenth to eleventh centuries BCE. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=7320" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:00</a> | Is there a correlation between the Sanhedrin's seventy members and the Septuagint's seventy scholars? | He says yes in the sense that seventy is a canonical perfection number built from seven. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=7440" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:00</a> | In Judges 8:24, why are Midianites called Ishmaelites? | He says Midianites were associated with Ishmaelite ancestry at that stage, and the wording may also reflect a gloss. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=7574" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:14</a> | Had Israel already stopped observing Jubilee before Saul? | He says yes; many prescribed observances had already broken down early in the judges period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=7668" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:07:48</a> | In Judges 8:27, did Israel worship the ephod? | He says they went astray over an ephod Gideon made, treating it as an idolatrous snare. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=7830" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:30</a> | Is "played the harlot with it" a metaphor, or were they literally doing that with the ephod? | He says it is a metaphor for idolatry. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=7877" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:17</a> | May I request a popcorn review of Brian Young and Ron Wyatt material? | He says he will consider it if the content fits the channel's copyright, format, and expertise constraints. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=8513" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:53</a> | In Judges 9:54, how disgraceful was it to die by a woman? | He says it was not a hero's death and was considered humiliating. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=8695" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:55</a> | Did the woman in Judges 9 intentionally try to kill Abimelech or just drop the millstone? | He says she was trying to hit someone from the tower, but likely got lucky with the exact strike. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=8782" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:22</a> | Why was it such an offense not to invite Ephraim to fight in Judges 12:1? | He says passing through another tribe's territory normally involved inviting them, and Ephraim also wanted the prestige and spoils. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=9019" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:19</a> | What about permissions just to pass through Ephraimite territory? | He says Jephthah probably did not ask and did not need permission because he was going to war against another people. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=9049" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:49</a> | How did ancient Israelites understand music? | He says they used it for worship, entertainment, and sometimes prophecy, not as something inherently divine in itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=9162" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:42</a> | Were ancient Jews involved in entertainment as they are today? | He says yes; music served entertainment as well as liturgical purposes, with David as an example. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=9272" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:32</a> | To what extent can modern reconstructions of Israelite music recover the real ancient sound? | He says only very imperfectly, because no notation or secure tunings survive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=9332" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:32</a> | In Judges 12:9, was Ibzan sinning by marrying his children to outsiders? | He says yes; he treats Ibzan as a bad judge making compromising foreign marriages. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=9419" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:59</a> | Who lived in Laish in Judges 18:27? | He says it was a Canaanite population tied to the Sidon and Tyre sphere. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=9549" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:09</a> | In Judges 19:22, why did the men want relations with the Levite? | He says it was a violent power display and evidence of how depraved Benjamin had become. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=9875" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:35</a> | Why did Benjamin defend the offenders with military force instead of extraditing them? | He says the whole episode is corrupt on multiple sides, but Benjamin chose tribal solidarity over justice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=9975" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:15</a> | In Judges 19:25, why did the Levite send his concubine out? | He says because he was a coward. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=10127" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:47</a> | In Judges 20:13, who is Belial and why "sons of Belial"? | He says it means worthless men, with wordplay linked to Baal language. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=10255" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:55</a> | In Judges 21:20-21, how could Benjamin steal daughters if Israel had vowed not to give them? | He says it was a technical loophole: they would not hand daughters over directly, but could allow a staged "abduction" scenario. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=10508" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:08</a> | How true is it that Canaanites, Amorites, and Habiru were the same people? | He says it is not true: Canaanite and Amorite are ethnic-historical categories, while Habiru is a social class. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=10729" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:49</a> | Are there books that explain prehistory such as Gobekli Tepe and the transition to Abrahamic times? | He says he does not know one he can recommend and doubts a single book could cover that whole span well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=10959" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:39</a> | In Ruth 2:14, what is bread dipped in vinegar? | He says it is basically bread with vinegar, likely also oil, and still resembles a familiar Mediterranean dip. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=11127" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:27</a> | In Ruth 3:4, did Naomi hope Boaz would sleep with Ruth so he would marry her? | He says yes; that is the implication of Naomi's plan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=11230" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:10</a> | Would the early Eucharist ever have used vinegar instead of wine? | He says no; vinegar would have been avoided because of its crucifixion association. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=11325" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:45</a> | Could Eucharist use actual bread instead of circular wafers? | He says yes; wafers are not required, and various breads have been used, though some traditions specify wheat. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=11556" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:36</a> | Is Moses a copy of Sargon? | He says no; apart from a basket and river, the narratives are too different and the surviving Sargon text is much later. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=11893" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:13</a> | Since Boaz was a kinsman-redeemer, is David technically descended from Mahlon or Elimelech rather than Boaz? | He says no; David's line is still reckoned through Boaz. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=11938" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:58</a> | In Ruth 3:17, is the barley a dowry for Naomi? | He says no; it is just provision for Naomi, not a dowry. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=12027" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:27</a> | In Ruth 4:1-2, does Boaz's role at the gate show he was highly respected? | He says yes; he likely functioned as a respected elder with wealth and standing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=12082" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:22</a> | In Ruth 4:3, is Boaz lying about the land? | He says no; he is describing a genuine kinsman-redemption situation involving Naomi's inheritance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=12239" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:59</a> | In Ruth 4:6, what was the nearer redeemer trying to protect? | He says he was protecting his own inheritance and posterity from a complicated marriage-and-land obligation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=12485" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:05</a> | Do people in the West misread Genesis 2:24? | He says they probably do sometimes, though he does not specify a single misreading beyond noting it grounds marriage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=12545" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:05</a> | In Ruth 4:11, why is everyone fine with Ruth being a Moabite? | He says the text reflects grace toward a righteous Gentile and perhaps some practical overlooking of the problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=12777" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:32:57</a> | Why was the ark stationed at Shiloh? | He says Shiloh was the one cult site all the tribes could agree on before Israel had a capital. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=12909" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:35:09</a> | In 1 Samuel 1:9, why is Shiloh called the temple of the Lord? | He says the Shiloh complex had grown beyond a simple tent into a real temple-like cult center. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=13061" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:41</a> | In 1 Samuel 1:12, was Eli the high priest? | He says yes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=13316" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:41:56</a> | How did Israel end up with no suitable high priest for a time? | He says Eli's line was effectively cut off when the men who should have served died before a qualified heir was grown. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=13501" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:45:01</a> | In 1 Samuel 2:10, why mention a king before Israel had one? | He says it is prophetic language anticipating a future king. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=13585" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:46:25</a> | In 1 Samuel 2:22, who were the women serving at the doorway? | He says they were women doing support work around the sanctuary complex. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=13648" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:47:28</a> | In 1 Samuel 3:7, what does it mean that Samuel did not yet know Yahweh? | He says it means Samuel had not yet personally encountered and recognized the Lord's voice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=13804" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:04</a> | Do you affirm the Theotokos? | He says yes, and calls it basic Christian orthodoxy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=13945" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:52:25</a> | Are angels still active in Christians' lives and in human affairs? | He says yes; they still guard, guide, and minister even if people rarely see them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=14075" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:35</a> | How should I understand *Elohim* when "God" is such a loaded word now? | He says context decides whether *Elohim* is a singular majestic term for God or a plural reference to gods or spiritual beings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/-OcrbEa6Lpk?t=14311" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:58:31</a> | In John 10:34-35, is Jesus identifying himself as the God who called them gods or as one of the ones called gods? | He says Jesus is turning Psalm 82 back on his opponents: if Scripture can use "gods" there, calling himself the Son of God is not blasphemy. |
